skill.name: scientific-podcast-summary
skill.description: Automatically summarize scientific podcasts like Huberman Lab and Nature.
skill.summary: Fetches podcast episodes and generates AI-powered structured summaries with key findings and actionable advice.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: This skill fetches podcast web content from Huberman Lab and Nature Podcast, sends it to an OpenAI-compatible LLM API for summarization, and writes output to files. All 85 static findings were evaluated: external_commands findings (40) are false positives from markdown documentation code blocks; network findings (9) are legitimate HTTP requests to known podcast sources; filesystem findings (2) are legitimate output file writes; env_access findings (13) are required for OpenAI API authentication. The skill has no evidence of malicious intent, data exfiltration, or command injection. Risk level is medium due to inherent network and credential access, but the skill is safe to publish with appropriate warnings.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Authentication
medium_findings.description: The skill reads OPENAI_API_KEY from environment variables and uses it to authenticate with the OpenAI API. This is legitimate functionality required for the skill's core purpose of LLM-based summarization, but API key handling should follow security best practices.

medium_findings.title: Network Requests to External Podcast Sources
medium_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to hubermanlab.com and nature.com to fetch podcast episode content. This is legitimate functionality for the skill's stated purpose, but users should be aware that network access is required.

medium_findings.title: File System Write Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill writes generated summaries to the file system using a user-specified output path. The content written is generated summary text, not sensitive data. Path traversal risk is minimal because the output path is controlled by the user via command-line argument.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Endpoints in Configuration
low_findings.description: The skill contains hardcoded URLs for Huberman Lab and Nature Podcast in the PODCAST_SOURCES configuration dictionary. These are legitimate public podcast source URLs. No security risk as they are well-known public endpoints.

low_findings.title: External Commands in Markdown Documentation - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged backtick usage in SKILL.md and references/audit-reference.md as shell command execution. These are all within markdown code blocks showing example commands for documentation purposes only. SKILL.md is a documentation file, not an executable script. No command injection risk.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Flag - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at several locations. No cryptographic operations exist in the code. The flagged locations contain regex patterns (e.g., re.compile patterns for HTML parsing) and URL path strings that the scanner incorrectly identified as cryptographic.

content.value_statement: Scientific podcasts contain valuable research insights but are time-consuming to listen to fully. This skill automatically fetches podcast episodes from Huberman Lab and Nature Podcast and generates structured summaries with key findings and actionable advice.
actual_capabilities: Fetch latest episode content from Huberman Lab podcast automatically
Fetch latest episode content from Nature Podcast automatically
Generate structured AI summaries with key scientific findings and practical advice
Output summaries in Markdown or JSON format
Save summaries to a specified file path on disk
Parse user-provided podcast episode URLs for custom episodes
limitations: Requires a valid OpenAI API key configured as an environment variable
Only supports Huberman Lab and Nature Podcast as sources
Summary quality depends on the capability of the LLM model used
Requires internet access to fetch podcast episode pages

use_cases.title: Stay Updated on Scientific Research
use_cases.description: Automatically pull and summarize the latest Nature Podcast episode to stay informed about new scientific discoveries without listening to the full audio.

use_cases.title: Quick Learning for Science Enthusiasts
use_cases.description: Get concise summaries of Huberman Lab episodes covering neuroscience and health topics. Extract key scientific points and practical advice in minutes.

use_cases.title: Content Curation for Science Communication
use_cases.description: Collect and organize summaries from multiple podcast episodes to create curated content briefs for newsletters, social media, or internal team updates.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize Latest Huberman Lab Episode
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize the latest episode of Huberman Lab for me.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants a quick overview of the most recent Huberman Lab podcast episode

prompt_templates.title: Summarize Nature Podcast with URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize the Nature Podcast episode at https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-024-00123-4 in markdown format and save it to ./nature-summary.md
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has a specific Nature Podcast article URL and wants a detailed summary saved to a file

prompt_templates.title: JSON Format Output for Data Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize the latest Huberman Lab episode in JSON format and output the result.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user needs structured JSON data for integration with other tools or automated workflows

prompt_templates.title: Batch Comparison of Multiple Episodes
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize the Nature Podcast episode at https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-024-00123-4 in JSON format. Then also summarize the Huberman Lab episode about sleep science. Compare the key research findings from both episodes.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher wants to compare findings across multiple podcast episodes from different sources

output_examples.input: Summarize the latest Huberman Lab episode.
output_examples.output:
# 🎙️ Huberman Lab: The Science of Sleep

**Release Date:** 2024-01-15
**Host:** Andrew Huberman
**Guest:** Dr. Matthew Walker

---

## Core Topic

This episode explores the neuroscience of sleep, including the mechanisms that regulate sleep cycles and the critical role of deep sleep for memory consolidation and brain health.

## Key Points
1. Humans experience 4-6 90-minute sleep cycles each night
2. During deep sleep, the brain clears metabolic waste products
3. Consistent sleep schedules are more important than total sleep duration

## Practical Advice
- Maintain a regular sleep schedule even on weekends
- Avoid blue light exposure 60 minutes before bed
- Keep bedroom temperature between 18-20 degrees Celsius

output_examples.input: Summarize the Nature Podcast episode at https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-024-00123-4 in JSON format.

best_practices: Verify the output summary contains accurate scientific information before sharing with others
Set the OPENAI_MODEL environment variable to a capable model like gpt-4 for better summary quality
Use the --output flag to save summaries to a file for future reference and documentation
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for podcasts outside of scientific topics, as the extraction logic is optimized for research content
Do not rely on the summary as a complete replacement for listening to the full episode, as important context may be lost
Do not share raw summary output without reviewing for accuracy and completeness

faq.question: What podcasts are supported?
faq.answer: Huberman Lab and Nature Podcast are the two supported sources.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need an OpenAI API key set as the OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Can I add support for other podcasts?
faq.answer: No, adding new podcast sources requires modifying the Python source code directly.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: Markdown and JSON formats are both supported via the --format argument.

faq.question: Does the skill work without internet access?
faq.answer: No, internet access is required to fetch podcast episode pages from the web.

faq.question: Is the summary as accurate as listening to the full episode?
faq.answer: No, summaries may miss important context, nuance, or detailed explanations from the original audio.
